site.title: MENDAKI Volunteer Hub
site.heroSubtitle: Join volunteers uplifting the Malay/Muslim community across Singapore.

about.intro: MENDAKI is Singapore's self-help group for the Malay/Muslim community since 1982. We advance education and community upliftment through four pillars: Confident Learners, Stronger Families, Future-Ready Workforce, and Thriving Communities.
about.whyVolunteer: Become part of a meaningful community. No prior experience needed - training and orientation are provided for all roles.

faq.question: Do I need to be Malay/Muslim to volunteer?
faq.answer: No - we welcome volunteers of all backgrounds who share our commitment to community upliftment.

faq.question: Is there a minimum age?
faq.answer: Most roles are open to those 18 and above. Some may accept 15-17 year olds with parental consent.

faq.question: Do I need teaching experience?
faq.answer: No. Training and orientation are provided before you begin any role. All you need is enthusiasm and commitment.

faq.question: How much time do I need?
faq.answer: Ad-hoc events need just a few hours. Long-term roles ask for 2-4 hours per session, typically monthly or bi-weekly.

faq.question: Will I receive a certificate?
faq.answer: Yes. Volunteers who complete their commitment receive a certificate of appreciation and are eligible for our annual Volunteer Recognition Awards.

opportunities.title: RSL Maths Explorer
opportunities.description: Support preschool-aged children and parents in fun, hands-on maths activities that build early numeracy skills. You will work alongside MENDAKI educators to guide families through structured play-based learning sessions.
opportunities.requirements: No prior teaching experience needed. Training provided. Comfortable working with young children aged 4-6.

opportunities.title: #amPowered Mentor
opportunities.description: Mentor a Malay/Muslim youth aged 15-18 to help them discover their strengths, build resilience, and navigate education and career pathways. Mentors meet their mentees at least twice a month.
opportunities.requirements: 18 years and above. Willingness to commit to a 6-month mentoring relationship. Passion for youth development.

opportunities.title: Befriender
opportunities.description: Provide companionship to MENDAKI beneficiaries - seniors, single parents, or individuals facing hardship - through regular visits or calls. Your presence can make a profound difference to someone facing isolation.
opportunities.requirements: Empathetic, patient, and a good listener. No specific qualifications required.

opportunities.title: MAP Packing Day
opportunities.description: Help pack resource materials, stationery, and goodie bags for students enrolled in the MENDAKI Achievement Programme. A fun, social volunteering session that makes a direct impact on students across Singapore.
opportunities.requirements: Able-bodied and willing to work in a packing environment. No experience needed.

opportunities.title: RSL Child Minder
opportunities.description: Provide attentive child-minding support during RSL parent workshops, so that parents can participate fully without worry. You will supervise and engage children with structured activities during the session.
opportunities.requirements: Patient and comfortable with young children. Basic first aid knowledge a plus but not required.

opportunities.title: Raikan Ilmu Usher
opportunities.description: Be part of MENDAKI's flagship Raikan Ilmu awards ceremony - an inspiring celebration of student achievement. As an usher, you will guide guests, manage registration, and help create a memorable event experience.
opportunities.requirements: Presentable and well-spoken. Comfortable in a formal event setting. Smart casual or formal attire required.
